nodes.label: Correction record / certification case law
nodes.summary: "the originating PDF's printed value is wrong from digit 40 (`…9694131413…` → certified `…9694121855…`). Root cause: mpmath's singular-path `ellippi(n>1, m)` loses ~25 internal digits. A session error of ours is owned in the same breath: an initial \"reproduces\" check compared two runs of the same singular path and missed that they disagreed at digit 40 — reproduction of an artifact is not certification of a value. Case law: **never certify from a singular-path special-function evaluation; always two independent routes.**"

nodes.label: Stage 1 residue theorem
nodes.summary:
"For `ω = P·dx/((1−n′x²)·y)` on `y² = (1−x²)(1−k′²x²)`, `P = −2^(7/4)(3−2√2)`, pole `x₀ = 1/√n′`:
x₀² = 12√2−16 ;  1−x₀² = (3−2√2)² ;  1−k′²x₀² = 3−2√2 ;  n′·(3−2√2) = √2/8
Res_bare(+x₀) = −2^(1/4)(3+2√2) ;   P·Res_bare = 4   (exactly)
Hand derivation plus machine proof: `minimal_polynomial(P·Res_bare) = x − 4` (sign carried; no branch ambiguity)."

nodes.label: Continuation-offset and pairwise-cancellation corollaries
nodes.summary: "Corollaries: one-sided continuation differs from CPV by ±4πi; the two continuations differ by 8πi; residues at the four curve points over ±x₀ cancel pairwise (gate G3b)."

nodes.label: Two-route CPV agreement certification
nodes.summary: "Two independent CPV routes — complex-contour and the regular interchange identity `PV Π(n′;m) = K(m) − Π(m/n′; m)` with `m/n′ = 2√2−2 < 1` — agree to `3.2e−136` at dps 270. The 220-digit pin above is two-route certified to that floor and single-route (regular path, dps-stable) beyond."

nodes.label: Paper-ready regular closed form for I_dual

extensions.tag_from_source: CERTIFIED (numerics, with scope) — the paper-ready regular closed form (no CPV, every parameter in (0,1))

nodes.label: Period controls (ω1, ω2, rhombic relation)
nodes.summary: "Period controls, gated against independent Carlson-RF computation (exact to working precision): `ω₁ = 2^(7/4)·K(k²)`, `Im ω₂ = −2^(3/4)·K(k′²)`, `Re ω₂ = ω₁/2` (rhombic). The 2^(7/4) scale in both constants is the period normalization."

nodes.label: Structural duality: shared j-invariant and Galois period swap
nodes.summary:
"λ(1−λ) = 1/8 for both moduli  ⟹  j(Legendre curve) = 10976 for primary AND dual
Φ₂(128, 10976) = 0            ⟹  2-isogenous to the minimal j = 128 model
λ ↦ 1−λ is an isomorphism      ⟹  primary/dual Legendre curves are THE SAME curve;
                                   Galois conjugation acts as the period swap"

nodes.label: Surface-to-link identity closing the primary geometric interface
nodes.summary: "the surface-to-link theorem proves \\int_{S_1}K_G\\,dA=-2L(\\Gamma_1^+)=I_{\\rm primary}. The proof constructs a global algebraic primitive for K_GdA, evaluates its two cone-end limits, and identifies the resulting density with the spherical-link speed by an exact substitution. The established link-to-Legendre reduction then yields the primary formula in §1."

nodes.label: Stage 2 open proof plan
nodes.summary: "Stage 2 of the proof plan: normalize ω as a third-kind differential on the λ-curve, Riemann bilinear relations with the Abel–Jacobi position of the pole divisor, transfer through the 2-isogeny to the j = 128 model. Only a module-level \"companion\" statement (third-kind periods + quasi-periods + residues) can survive §5's refutation."

nodes.label: Safer theorem statement summary table
nodes.summary:
"PROVEN:   I_primary is the original DBP total-curvature integral; the branch structure of I_dual — residue 4, offsets ±4πi / 8πi.
CERTIFIED: I_primary period evaluation, I_dual_CPV (two-route), the regular form, the period identities, j/isogeny structure.
CANDIDATE: I_dual as a principal-value third-kind companion on the conjugate reduction (Stage 2 to make precise).
NOT CLAIMED: lattice completion."

nodes.label: Exact upper and lower DBP corridor lifts
nodes.summary: Let U^o=C\{0,+i,-i} and rho^2=1+sigma^2. The rational polygon gamma_up with vertices (1,0),(1/2,1),(1/2,3/2),(-1/2,3/2),(-1/2,1/2),(1/2,1/2),(1/2,1),(1,0), and its conjugate gamma_down, lift uniquely from (sigma,rho)=(1,+sqrt(2)) to (1,-sqrt(2)). Their reduced words are a_+ and a_-^(-1), their winding vectors are (0,1,0) and (0,0,-1), and the open radius-1/8 tubes avoid 0,+i,-i.

nodes.label: DBP corridor lateral pole-side calibration
nodes.summary: On the upper return stem sigma(h)=1+h(-1/2+i), 0<h<=1, the continued terminal sheet has Im(rho)<0 and therefore Im(m)<0 for m=1/2-1/(2rho); the conjugate lower stem has Im(m)>0. Thus upper selects delta_-^up and lower selects delta_-^down on the exact interval h_0=1.

nodes.label: DBP corridor divisor reduction in Q(rho)
Exact curve-divisor bounds on DBP corridors and radius-1/8 tubes
nodes.summary: On either released route, conservative lower bounds include |rho+/-1|>=1/32, |m|,|1-m|>=1/192, |n|,|1-n|>=1/12288, |x_p-m|>=1/24576, and |y_p^2|>=1/6291456. On the radius-1/8 tube the corresponding bounds are 1/64, 1/512, 1/65536, 1/163840, and 1/131072000.

nodes.label: Relative homology transport along the two exact DBP corridors
nodes.summary: Modulo Lambda_ell=Z[A]+Z[B], G_up[delta_+]=[delta_-^up] and G_down[delta_+]=[delta_-^down]. Reversal of the odd local branch meridian changes only the compact Picard-Lefschetz correction at this quotient level.

nodes.label: Endpoint relative-class basis for the DBP Legendre third-kind family
nodes.summary: At u_-=(sigma=1,rho=-sqrt(2)), use the ordered basis (A_-,B_-,mu_-,delta_-^up), compact intersection matrix [[0,1],[-1,0]], boundary vector (0,0,0,1), compact submodule Z[A_-]+Z[B_-], and quotient basis (mu_-,delta_-^up).

nodes.label: DBP lateral and CPV class coordinates
nodes.summary: In the basis (A_-,B_-,mu_-,delta_-^up), delta_up=(0,0,0,1), delta_down=(0,0,1,1), delta_up-delta_down=-mu, and delta_CPV=(delta_up+delta_down)/2=(0,0,1/2,1). In the compact quotient their coordinates are (0,1), (1,1), and (1/2,1).

nodes.label: Minimal coefficient-ring diamond for DBP relative classes
nodes.summary: The legal coefficient rings form the diamond Z -> Z[1/2], Z -> Z[i], Z[1/2] -> Z[1/2,i], Z[i] -> Z[1/2,i]. The minimal rings are Z for delta_up and delta_down, Z[1/2] for delta_CPV, Z[i] for i delta_up and i delta_down, and Z[1/2,i] for i delta_CPV.

nodes.label: Boundary obstruction to an integral replacement for Gaussian phase
nodes.summary: For a relative class with primitive boundary beta, partial(i delta)=i beta lies outside Z beta, and adding closed cycles changes the boundary by zero. Hence multiplication by i cannot be replaced by an integral relative class operation.

nodes.label: Arbitrary-precision bounded evaluation of the two DBP observables
nodes.summary: For each target in {primary, dual_cpv} and every integer target_bits>=16, the finite six-operation kernel grammar produces an exact dyadic bracket meeting the requested width. The primary normalization retains the -2 pi enclosure, while the dual certificate uses exact zero CPV of the polar kernel and no runtime pole crossing.

nodes.label: Generic compositional semantics for arbitrary six-opcode period programs
nodes.summary: Arbitrary programs remain open because generic compositional complex-disk branch and denominator witnesses, together with a program-derived Cauchy-tail theorem, have not been constructed; relaxing sealed-spec equality alone would execute the wrong fixed kernel.

nodes.label: Marked relative Legendre fibre for the DBP K/E/Pi connection
nodes.summary: In u=x/m coordinates the ordered branch sections are 0,1,q=1/m,infinity and the selected third-kind pole is p=1/n. The marking has A equal to the doubled selected lift [0,1], B the doubled arc [1,q] with holomorphic period +2iK(1-m), mu the positive pole meridian, delta the selected lateral lift, A.B=+1, and partial(A,B,mu,delta)=(0,0,0,beta).

nodes.label: Exact signed branch crossing on the DBP corridors
nodes.summary: Along the upper corridor q=1/m crosses (0,1) once from the lower to the upper half-plane; the lower conjugate corridor crosses from upper to lower. The crossing occurs at sigma=i/2, rho=-sqrt(3)/2, q=4sqrt(3)-6 in (0,1), with signed crossing numbers +1 and -1.

nodes.label: Absolute Picard-Lefschetz calibration for the two DBP corridors
nodes.summary: The compact actions are G_up(A)=A+2B, G_up(B)=B and G_down(A)=A-2B, G_down(B)=B. On the relative contour, G_up(delta_+)=delta_-^up+B_- and G_down(delta_+)=delta_-^down-B_-, so lambda_up=B_- and lambda_down=-B_-.

nodes.label: DBP absolute relative transport matrices and CPV cancellation
nodes.summary: In route-specific bases (A_-,B_-,mu_-,delta_-^up/down), the column matrices are M_up=[[1,0,0,0],[2,1,0,1],[0,0,1,0],[0,0,0,1]] and M_down=[[1,0,0,0],[-2,1,0,-1],[0,0,1,0],[0,0,0,1]]. They give G_up(delta_+)-G_down(delta_+)=2B_--mu_- and (G_up(delta_+)+G_down(delta_+))/2=delta_-^CPV.

nodes.label: Holomorphic period continuation under the calibrated DBP braid
nodes.summary: Under the chosen orientation, K(m_+) continues to K(m_-) +/- 2iK(1-m_-), the period realization of A -> A +/- 2B.

nodes.label: Fixed-curve trace-path comparison on E_128
nodes.summary: With A_E the primitive real-projective-circle lift and mu_E the positive pole meridian, [Gamma_-^up]=[Gamma_+]+[A_E] and [Gamma_-^down]=[Gamma_+]+[A_E]+[mu_E]. Thus the upper comparison coordinates are (a,b,c)=(1,0,0), while the lower meridian coordinate is c+1=1.

nodes.label: Local residues of the DBP K/E/Pi connection
Non-logarithmic m=1 germ of the DBP connection
nodes.summary: At m=1+x, M(1+x)=N_{-2}/x^2+N_{-1}/x+O(1), with N_{-2} having sole nonzero entry (3,2)=3/2 and N_{-1}=[[0,-1/2,0],[0,0,0],[1,1/2,-3/2]]. Therefore naive exponentiation of a residue at m=1 is invalid in the current basis.

nodes.label: Integral geometric calibration of the full regular DBP parameter-plane groupoid
nodes.summary: Construct a source-compatible rational or meromorphic gauge at m=1 reducing the nilpotent double-pole germ to a proved normal form while retaining the integral marking, then supply based cleared loops, local-to-global marking comparisons, integral monodromy matrices, and fundamental-group relations.

nodes.label: Native DBP surface-sweep discriminant and norm-polar resultant
nodes.summary: For N=(T-c^2V)Xi^2+(1-c)TH^2 and R=Xi^2+H^2, Disc_Xi(N)=-4(1-c)T(T-c^2V) and Res_[Xi:H](N,R)=c^2(T-cV)^2.

nodes.label: Reduction of DBP surface-sweep base divisors to marked curve divisors
nodes.summary: With x=2BV/Delta_2, m=B/(2rho), x_p=-2/B, the surface divisors V=0,V=T,T=0,T-cV=0,T-c^2V=0 correspond respectively to x=0,x=m,x=x_p,x=1,x=infinity. Thus the three excluded surface divisors are exactly the transported pole, branch point, and infinity.

nodes.label: Uniform exact exclusion radius for the released DBP surface sweeps
nodes.summary: The five projective base points P_0=[0:1], P_m=[1:1], P_infinity=[1:0], P_1=[1:c], P_2=[1:c^2] have conservative pairwise chordal separation at least 1/26296576800. Hence epsilon_*=1/105186307200 gives disjoint exclusion neighborhoods and |T|,|T-cV|,|T-c^2V|>=epsilon_* along either released refined track.

nodes.label: Quantitative curve-to-surface clearance transfer
nodes.summary: If |T|>=epsilon_infinity, |T-cV|>=epsilon_1, and |T-c^2V|>=epsilon_2, then |Disc_Xi(N)|>=(8/5)epsilon_infinity epsilon_2, |Res(N,R)|>=epsilon_1^2/102400, |(1-c)T|>=(2/5)epsilon_infinity, and |T-c^2V|>=epsilon_2.

nodes.label: Exact clearance transfer and naturality on the native DBP swept image
nodes.summary: For either exact lifted corridor, the refined representative transports in the complement of x_p,1,infinity; its four-quadrant angular lift and relative norm-cover fibre interval are collision-free; the swept chains form an integral isotopy in H_2(X_u,Y_u;Z); and G_eta^S L_{Z,u+}=L_{Z,u-}G_eta^C for eta in {up,down} on im L_Z.

nodes.label: Relative homology rank of the projective DBP norm-cover complement
nodes.summary: For the smooth (2,2) complete-intersection surface S, X=S\D_q and Y=D_infinity\(D_q intersect D_infinity), one has H_2(X;Z)=Z^7, H_1(Y;Z)=Z^5, and the split relative exact sequence gives H_2(X,Y;Z)=Z^12.

nodes.label: Native DBP swept transfer is not whole-surface surjective
nodes.summary: The source H_1(C^o,{Q_0,Q_m};Z) has rank four, R_Z L_Z=4 id makes L_Z injective on its admissible source, and H_2(X,Y;Z) has rank twelve. Therefore im L_Z is not H_2(X,Y;Z), with at least eight independent whole-surface directions outside the native carrier.

nodes.label: Saturation of the native DBP rank-four surface sublattice
nodes.summary: Determine the Smith normal form of im L_Z -> H_2(X,Y;Z), equivalently an integral intersection matrix for the four native generators against a dual basis or integral functionals detecting divisibility of L_Z(mu). Whole-lattice CPV non-descent remains open although whole-surface surjectivity is false.

nodes.label: Static strict-chamber exact root transport and contact-wall specialization
nodes.summary: Exact rational polygonal continuation is valid throughout the full static strict chamber. Concavity checks the chamber inequality on every segment, affine nonvanishing of N_i-N_j and N_i+N_j checks H2, Sturm isolators transport fixed-charge routes, and charge-magnitude ordering supplies labels on multivariable routes. The wall classifier separates simple physical contact from zero-charge signed-sheet coincidence.

nodes.label: Complex loop and braid realization on the normalized incidence cover
nodes.summary: Construct exact based complex loop representatives, execute their braids upstairs on the normalized incidence cover, prove refinement independence, and compare the resulting action with the calibrated DBP route groupoid.

nodes.label: Finite-tower naturality of exact role recharts
nodes.summary: Let J_N=Q[D,S]_{>=1}/(D,S)^{N+1} and tau_{N,r}:J_N->J_r for 2<=r<=N. On the regular active-role locus, both exact generators s,t commute with truncation; hence tau_{N,r}(w f)=w tau_{N,r}(f) for every finite word w in <s,t>. This is a projective-system theorem and does not assert analytic convergence of an infinite germ.

nodes.label: No unreduced full-carrier equivalence among role, horizon, and elliptic fibres
nodes.summary: A structure-preserving full-carrier equivalence is false: the role, five-sheet horizon, and rank-four elliptic fibres have incompatible dimension or cardinality, isotropy, and monodromy. Any remaining equivalence target must first reduce each realization to a common selected skeleton.

nodes.label: Off-diagonal role-rechart covariance of the curvature Laurent laws
nodes.summary: The arbitrary-transverse diagonal-germ result does not prove full role-rechart covariance; an off-diagonal tensor pullback, divisor-unit law, curvature replay, and corner-incidence transport remain required.

nodes.label: Exact lossless two-bus AC-fold cover
nodes.summary: For rational P,Q,X,E with X,E>0, F(v)=v^2+(2QX-E^2)v+X^2(P^2+Q^2) has discriminant Delta=E^4-4E^2QX-4P^2X^2. On Delta>0 its normalized sheets are v_+=(E^2-2QX+sqrt(Delta))/2 and v_-=(E^2-2QX-sqrt(Delta))/2; the no-load physical section selects the high-voltage sheet v=E^2.

nodes.label: Winding groupoid and parity reduction of the AC fold
nodes.summary: Native morphisms carry integer winding about Delta=0; composition adds winding, reversal negates it, and the target sheet swaps exactly for odd winding. The reduction on morphisms is Z->C_2, k->k mod 2. It is full and essentially surjective onto its declared two-sheet parity image but is not faithful, with kernel 2Z.

nodes.label: Precommitted exact AC-fold root fixture
nodes.summary: At (P,Q,X,E)=(1,0,1,2), F(v)=v^2-4v+1, Delta=12, v_high=2+sqrt(3) lies in (3,4), and v_low=2-sqrt(3) lies in (0,1). One loop swaps the roots and two loops fix them.

nodes.label: DBP-arm reduction functors and three-way selected-skeleton coherence
nodes.summary: Construct the two DBP-arm functors into the same typed selected skeleton used by the AC fold, then prove componentwise well-definedness, fullness, faithfulness, essential surjectivity, and the comparison diagrams. The AC-fold construction alone does not establish a three-way natural isomorphism.
